attributes.description: Alerts when replica `time_behind_source > 60` seconds. Stale replicas risk read-after-write issues and data loss on failover.
attributes.name: [MySQL OTel] Replication lag

investigation_guide.blob:
## MySQL replication lag

### What fired
`mysql.replica.time_behind_source > 60s` during the window.

### Why it matters
Replication lag directly exposes stale reads from replicas and risks data loss during failover.

### Triage
1. Check network between primary and replica.
2. Inspect replica CPU / disk.
3. Look for long-running transactions on the primary.

### Remediation
- Scale replica or use parallel SQL threads.
- Break up long-running transactions.

### Tuning
- 60s threshold; tune to RPO.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-mysqlreceiver.otel-*
// Replica lag is a gauge (seconds behind source); only present on replica instances
| WHERE metrics.mysql.replica.time_behind_source IS NOT NULL
| STATS lag_seconds = MAX(metrics.mysql.replica.time_behind_source)
  BY resource.attributes.mysql.instance.endpoint
// Alert when lag exceeds 60 seconds; adjust for your RPO requirements
| WHERE lag_seconds > 60
| SORT lag_seconds DESC
